title: SemanticAddress
description: The (context, intent, value) coordinate of any artifact in the development sheaf. Every artifact, every routing decision, every speculative reject has an address in this space. Proximity in this space = relevance. Distance = irrelevance. Persistence across time = truth.

scope.description: Hierarchical position in the task tree. Determines which fiber of the sheaf this artifact lives in.

timestamp.description: Temporal coordinate — when this artifact was produced. Enables meta-persistence across time.

context.description: WHAT the world looks like at this point. The base space of the fiber bundle.

codebase_hash.description: Git commit hash — snapshot of the world

dependencies.description: Semantic addresses of artifacts this depends on

intent.description: WHY this operation exists. The connection on the fiber bundle — defines valid transport.

request.description: The user's original request (scoped, not the full conversation)

purpose.description: What this artifact is FOR in the pipeline

value.description: WHAT truth this delivers. The section of the sheaf — the actual content at this fiber.

artifact_type.description: The typed artifact this address points to

artifact_path.description: File path to the artifact JSON

truth_claim.description: One-sentence summary of what this artifact asserts about the world

confidence.description: How persistent this truth is — bar_length normalized to [0,1]. Long bar = high confidence.

confirmed_by.description: Semantic addresses of artifacts that validated this one (L3 verdicts, test results, etc.)
